players.name: Max Crocombe
players.bio: Auckland-born shot-stopper who kept goal in the Oceania qualifiers with steady hands and nerves of steel. The Millwall keeper brings Championship-level experience to New Zealand's last line of defence at 32.
players.facts: Born on 12 August 1993 — same day as a very different kind of All White: the surrender of the last Japanese soldier, Hiroo Onoda.

players.name: Tim Payne
players.bio: A loyal servant of the All Whites with over 50 caps, Tim Payne brings dependable defensive quality and a winning mentality shaped by years at Wellington Phoenix. Not just a coffee order — this Tim delivers.
players.facts: With 51 caps, Payne has played more international football than most NZ defenders dare dream of — and scored 3 goals from the back.

players.name: Francis de Vries
players.bio: A composed defender with 20 caps to his name, Francis de Vries brings local Auckland FC know-how to the international stage. Calm in possession and solid in the tackle, he's a reliable cog in the All Whites backline.
players.facts: De Vries plays for Auckland FC — NZ's newest A-League club — making him a pioneer of Kiwi football's domestic revival.

players.name: Tyler Bindon
players.bio: Born in 2005, Tyler Bindon is one of the youngest players at the 2026 World Cup. The Sheffield United youngster has already amassed 25 caps and 3 international goals — a remarkable feat before his 22nd birthday.
players.facts: Bindon was born the same year YouTube launched. He's been scoring international goals almost as long as the internet has had cat videos.

players.name: Michael Boxall
players.bio: A rock-solid veteran with 63 caps, Michael Boxall is the heartbeat of New Zealand's defence. The Minnesota United centre-back is 37 and still bossing backlines — proof that some things, like fine wine and Boxall, improve with age.
players.facts: At 37, Boxall is the oldest outfield player in the squad — and arguably still its most commanding defender.

players.name: Joe Bell
players.bio: A composed, technically gifted midfielder who plies his trade in Norway's Eliteserien with Viking. Joe Bell reads the game well and acts as a crucial link between defence and attack for the All Whites at just 27.
players.facts: Bell plays for a club called Viking — which is arguably the most metal team name in world football. Thor would approve.

players.name: Matthew Garbett
players.bio: London-born but proudly Kiwi, Matthew Garbett is a dynamic central midfielder with an eye for goal. At just 24, the Peterborough United man has already scored 5 times for NZ — a serious return from midfield.
players.facts: Garbett was born in London but chose the All Whites — proving New Zealand's talent pipeline now runs through the English capital.

players.name: Marko Stamenić
players.bio: Wellington-born with a surname that sounds like a Champions League anthem, Marko Stamenić is a composed box-to-box midfielder who has impressed at Swansea City. Already 39 caps at 24 — the future is very much now.
players.facts: Stamenić has Croatian heritage — fitting for a player whose surname sounds like it belongs on a European tournament poster.

players.name: Chris Wood
players.bio: New Zealand's all-time great and talisman, Chris Wood is closing in on a century of caps with 45 international goals. The Nottingham Forest striker has scored at the top of the English game for over a decade. Simply irreplaceable.
players.facts: Wood became NZ's all-time top scorer years ago and shows no signs of stopping. At 34, he's still the first name on the teamsheet.

players.name: Sarpreet Singh
players.bio: The son of a former NZ international, Sarpreet Singh carries footballing royalty in his DNA. An inventive attacking midfielder who had a spell at Bayern Munich's reserve side, he's the creative heartbeat of the All Whites.
players.facts: Singh trained at Bayern Munich — making him the only All White who can say they've shared a dressing room with Bundesliga legends.

players.name: Elijah Just
players.bio: Palmerston North's finest export, Elijah Just is a pacy right winger who has carved out a solid career in Scottish football with Motherwell. Nine international goals from the wing makes him a genuine attacking threat.
players.facts: Just's name causes constant confusion in press boxes — reporters unsure if 'Elijah Just scored' is a sentence or a philosophical statement.

players.name: Alex Paulsen
players.bio: Young goalkeeper Alex Paulsen is making waves in Polish football with Lechia Gdańsk. With just 8 caps so far, this 23-year-old is the All Whites' keeper of the future — learning his trade far from home.
players.facts: Paulsen plays in Gdańsk, Poland — a city famous for shipbuilding, amber, and now apparently producing fine Kiwi goalkeepers.

players.name: Liberato Cacace
players.bio: With a name as bold as his overlapping runs, Liberato Cacace is a marauding left-back who has earned cult status at Wrexham. The 25-year-old brings energy, pace, and flair to everything he does for the All Whites.
players.facts: Cacace plays for Wrexham — the Hollywood-owned Welsh club — making him possibly the only All White with a movie star as a co-owner.

players.name: Alex Rufer
players.bio: The son of NZ football legend Wynton Rufer, Alex carries a famous footballing surname with admirable composure. A steady midfielder for Wellington Phoenix and the All Whites, he's keeping the family legacy alive.
players.facts: Alex's father Wynton Rufer won the UEFA Cup with Werder Bremen in 1992 — NZ football royalty doesn't get more regal than that.

players.name: Nando Pijnaker
players.bio: Dutch-born centre-back Nando Pijnaker brings European defensive solidity to the All Whites cause. Now plying his trade for Auckland FC, the 27-year-old has been a consistent presence in New Zealand's backline with 25 caps.
players.facts: Pijnaker was born in the Netherlands but chose the All Whites — following a well-worn path of Dutch-Kiwi dual nationals turning silver.

players.name: Finn Surman
players.bio: A young, energetic defender who has made his mark in MLS with Portland Timbers, Finn Surman brings athleticism and ambition to the All Whites setup. Two international goals from defence at 22 shows his attacking instincts.
players.facts: Surman plays in Portland — home of the world's most bookshops per capita. Whether he's read any is between him and his agent.

players.name: Kosta Barbarouses
players.bio: A veteran winger with 76 caps and a decade of loyal All Whites service, Kosta Barbarouses is pace, trickery, and experience wrapped in one. Now at Western Sydney Wanderers, he remains a dangerous weapon off the bench.
players.facts: Barbarouses has been terrorising full-backs for NZ since 2010 — his career has spanned four different World Cup cycles.

players.name: Ben Waine
players.bio: Wellington-born Ben Waine is a powerful centre-forward with an instinct for goal — nine international strikes in 31 caps is a fine record. The Port Vale man brings physicality and finishing to New Zealand's attack.
players.facts: Waine was born on 11 June 2001 — the exact day the 2026 World Cup kicks off. Happy birthday, Ben. What a way to celebrate.

players.name: Ben Old
players.bio: A composed left-back who has made the leap to French football with Saint-Étienne, Ben Old is one of NZ's most exciting young defenders. Auckland-born with two international goals, he adds creativity from the back.
players.facts: Old plays in France's Ligue 1 for Saint-Étienne — one of the few All Whites you might spot on a French football highlight reel.

players.name: Callum McCowatt
players.bio: An intelligent attacking midfielder with a sharp eye for goal, Callum McCowatt has flourished in Danish football with Silkeborg. Four goals in 32 caps for the All Whites shows his quality in the final third.
players.facts: McCowatt plays in Denmark — a country so passionate about football that even their pastries are called 'Danish.' Coincidence? Probably.

players.name: Jesse Randall
players.bio: A lively left winger with two goals from just 11 caps, Jesse Randall is one of the most exciting young talents in the All Whites squad. The Wellington-born Auckland FC man is here to make his World Cup mark.
players.facts: Randall's 11 caps mark him as the least experienced winger in the squad — but at a World Cup, reputations count for nothing.

players.name: Michael Woud
players.bio: Auckland-born Michael Woud is a commanding goalkeeper developing his craft at Auckland FC. With just 6 caps, the 27-year-old is very much the understudy at this tournament — but one with serious potential.
players.facts: Woud has 6 caps and plays domestically in NZ's A-League — making him the only goalkeeper in the squad yet to play in Europe.

players.name: Ryan Thomas
players.bio: A combative and technically capable midfielder, Ryan Thomas has built a steady All Whites career with 25 caps and 3 goals. His experience in Dutch football with PEC Zwolle adds European nous to New Zealand's engine room.
players.facts: Thomas plays in Zwolle — a Dutch city best known for its medieval fortifications. Ryan, however, is more of an attacking threat.

players.name: Callan Elliot
players.bio: A dependable defensive option with 11 caps, Callan Elliot provides cover and composure in the Auckland FC backline. The 26-year-old is a squad player who can be trusted when called upon at this World Cup.
players.facts: Elliot's birthday is 7/7/1999 — triple sevens, which in some cultures means extraordinary luck. Handy at a World Cup.

players.name: Lachlan Bayliss
players.bio: A fresh face in the All Whites setup with just 4 caps, Lachlan Bayliss is a midfield prospect gaining experience at the Newcastle Jets in Australia. Making a World Cup squad this young is already a remarkable achievement.
players.facts: Bayliss has just 4 caps — meaning this World Cup could easily double his entire international career before the group stage ends.

players.name: Tommy Smith
players.bio: Macclesfield-born but All White through and through, Tommy Smith is a seasoned centre-back with 56 caps and bags of experience. Now at Braintree Town at 36, he's a veteran leader marshalling New Zealand's defence.
players.facts: Smith plays for Braintree Town — one of the lowest-ranked clubs at any World Cup. From Essex non-league to global stage. Remarkable.
